skill.name: vector-database-engineer
skill.description: Guides vector database architecture, embedding strategy, index tuning, hybrid search, and retrieval design for RAG and similarity search systems.
skill.summary: Plan and optimize vector database systems for semantic search, RAG, and recommendations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown text and ordinary vector database terminology. No executable code, network access, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk due to the false-positive static matches and a non-security missing resource reference.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Span Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 20 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The line is Markdown text that names a documentation path to open for examples, not an executable command or shell expansion.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown formatting around a path. There is no script, interpreter call, command substitution, or user input execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Plain Text
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at lines 3 and 45. These lines contain front matter and workflow prose about descriptions and design, not cryptographic code or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is documentation content in SKILL.md. No hashing, encryption, crypto library use, or security-sensitive algorithm choice appears in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Search Terminology
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance at lines 27 and 48. These lines describe hybrid search as a vector database capability and workflow step, not host inspection or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references are domain terms for combining vector and keyword retrieval. No commands, APIs, or instructions collect host, network, user, process, or filesystem information.

content.value_statement: Vector search projects often fail because data shape, embeddings, indexes, and filters are designed separately. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan coherent retrieval systems with practical tuning guidance.
actual_capabilities: Compare Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, and pgvector for retrieval workloads.
Plan embedding model selection based on data type, dimensions, cost, and latency needs.
Recommend chunking, overlap, metadata, and filtering strategies for document search.
Explain index choices such as HNSW, IVF, and product quantization tradeoffs.
Guide hybrid vector and keyword search design for RAG and recommendations.
Suggest monitoring, caching, reindexing, and recall testing practices.
limitations: It provides guidance and plans, not a complete production implementation by itself.
It does not include the referenced implementation playbook file in this package.
It cannot benchmark latency or recall without representative data and runtime access.
It does not replace vendor documentation for account setup, quotas, or managed service limits.

use_cases.title: Plan a RAG Retrieval Layer
use_cases.description: Design chunking, embeddings, metadata filters, and vector database choices before implementation.

use_cases.title: Tune Search Quality
use_cases.description: Review recall, latency, index settings, and hybrid search options for an existing semantic search system.

use_cases.title: Choose a Vector Database
use_cases.description: Compare managed and self-hosted options against scale, filtering, operations, and budget constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Starting Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new semantic search feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a vector search architecture for my application. Ask for the data type, query patterns, scale, freshness needs, and deployment constraints before recommending options.

prompt_templates.title: Design a RAG Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a RAG indexing plan for my document collection. Include chunking strategy, embedding model criteria, metadata fields, filtering approach, and evaluation metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Retrieval Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user tuning an existing system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current vector search setup and propose changes to improve recall and latency. Consider index configuration, hybrid search, metadata filters, caching, and reranking.

prompt_templates.title: Scale a Production Vector System
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop a scaling plan for millions of vectors with frequent updates. Cover database selection, sharding or partitioning, reindexing, monitoring, drift detection, and cost controls.

output_examples.input: I need semantic search over product manuals for customer support.
output: A retrieval architecture with document parsing, chunking, embeddings, vector storage, metadata filters, and optional reranking.
A comparison of pgvector, Qdrant, and managed services based on expected scale and operations needs.
An evaluation plan using labeled support questions, recall checks, and latency targets.

output_examples.input: Our vector search is fast but misses relevant results.
output: A quality review focused on chunk size, embedding model fit, index recall settings, filters, and hybrid keyword search.
A test plan that compares recall before and after each tuning change.
Operational advice for monitoring drift and scheduling reindexing.

best_practices: Start with retrieval evaluation data before tuning indexes or changing embeddings.
Store metadata that supports filtering, access control, freshness, and diagnostics.
Measure recall, latency, cost, and update behavior with realistic queries.
anti_patterns: Choosing a vector database before defining query patterns and scale requirements.
Using fixed chunk sizes without validating retrieval quality on real tasks.
Ignoring metadata filters, access control, and reindexing plans until production.

faq.question: Which vector databases does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, and pgvector at a planning and tuning level.

faq.question: Can it write production-ready ingestion code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation steps, but this package only contains the core skill instructions.

faq.question: Does it help with hybrid search?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for combining vector retrieval with keyword search and metadata filtering.

faq.question: Is it useful for recommendation systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help design similarity search patterns for recommendations when embeddings are appropriate.

faq.question: Can it optimize an existing vector index?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest tuning steps for recall, latency, caching, filtering, and reindexing.

faq.question: Does it require network or system access?
faq.answer: No. The skill text provides guidance and does not require commands, network calls, or environment access.
